substitution_note: Substitute for 'proximal operator nonexpansiveness' since optlib has no nonexpansive theorem; this is the foundational subdifferential characterization at the same difficulty.
natural_language_statement: For a convex f : E → ℝ on a complete real inner product space, u is a proximal point of x with respect to f (i.e., u minimizes f(·) + ‖· - x‖^2 / 2) if and only if x - u is a subgradient of f at u.
assumptions: E is a complete real inner product space
conclusion: ∀ u : E, prox_prop f x u ↔ x - u ∈ SubderivAt f u
proof_sketch: Unfold prox_prop: u is a minimizer of f(·) + ‖· - x‖^2/2 iff 0 is in the subdifferential of (f + g) at u, where g(z) = ‖z - x‖^2 / 2.
g is convex (on univ) since norm-square is convex and shifting by x preserves convexity.
By subderivative additivity (SubderivAt.add): SubderivAt (f + g) u = SubderivAt f u + SubderivAt g u.
Compute SubderivAt g u: g has gradient g'(z) = z - x (gradient_of_sq), so its subderiv at u is the singleton {u - x}.
Therefore 0 ∈ SubderivAt (f + g) u iff -(u - x) = x - u ∈ SubderivAt f u.
